skill.name: competitive-ads-extractor
skill.description: Extracts and analyzes competitors' ads from ad libraries (Facebook, LinkedIn, etc.) to understand what messaging, problems, and creative approaches are working. Helps inspire and improve your own ad campaigns.
skill.summary: Extracts and analyzes competitors' ads from ad libraries (Facebook, LinkedIn, etc.) to understand wh...
skill.author: ComposioHQ

security_audit.summary: Static command execution and weak cryptography findings were false positives from markdown examples and ordinary text. The skill is non-executable guidance, but it asks assistants to access public ad libraries and save extracted outputs, so publication should note network and filesystem use.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Examples Flagged as Command Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer matched fenced markdown prompt examples and sample output as Ruby shell backtick execution. These blocks contain natural language prompts and illustrative reports, not executable Ruby or shell code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched locations are inside markdown code fences that show user prompts or expected output. No executable script, interpreter invocation, or command substitution syntax is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Keyword Match
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings appear to match ordinary words in descriptive markdown, not calls to MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or other cryptographic APIs. No evidence found of cryptographic implementation or credential handling.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are prose about ad analysis and ethical use. They contain no crypto library usage, hashing function, encryption mode, key material, or security-sensitive data flow.

content.user_title: Analyze Competitor Ads for Campaign Insights
content.value_statement: Competitive ad research is slow when ads, screenshots, and messages are reviewed one by one. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through extraction, comparison, and insight summaries.
actual_capabilities: Guides extraction of ads from public ad libraries such as Facebook and LinkedIn.
Captures screenshots and organizes visual references for later review.
Analyzes messaging, pain points, value propositions, CTAs, and creative formats.
Compares multiple competitors to identify repeated themes and positioning gaps.
Produces analysis reports, spreadsheets, presentations, and pattern libraries.
limitations: It depends on access to public ad libraries and available ad metadata.
It cannot verify true ad performance unless the platform exposes performance data.
It should not be used to copy protected ad creative or text.
Scraping behavior must follow each platform's terms and access rules.

use_cases.title: Competitor Messaging Review
use_cases.description: Map competitor pain points, positioning, and calls to action before planning a campaign.

use_cases.title: Creative Pattern Research
use_cases.description: Collect screenshots and group ads by format, visual style, and repeated creative themes.

use_cases.title: Market Positioning Benchmark
use_cases.description: Compare several companies to find common claims, underserved angles, and differentiated messages.

prompt_templates.title: Extract One Competitor
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner research on a single company.
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract current ads from [Competitor] in Facebook Ad Library. Summarize the main messages, CTAs, and creative formats.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze One Theme
prompt_templates.scenario: Focused review of a specific buyer problem.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review ads from [Competitor] that mention [problem]. Identify the pain points, proof points, and offer angles they use.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Competitors
prompt_templates.scenario: Campaign planning across a competitive set.
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract ads from [Company A], [Company B], and [Company C]. Compare their positioning, repeated themes, and creative formats.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Trend Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare [Competitor]'s ads from [period one] and [period two]. Explain what changed in messaging, audience focus, and format mix.

output_examples.input: Analyze Notion ads from Facebook Ad Library.
output_examples.output: A concise report showing total active ads, dominant themes, key pain points, common CTAs, creative patterns, and campaign recommendations.

output_examples.input: Compare ads from three project management tools.
output_examples.output: A competitor matrix that highlights shared claims, unique positioning, audience segments, and gaps for original campaign ideas.

output_examples.input: Review video ads versus static image ads for one competitor.
output_examples.output: A format analysis that explains how each format presents problems, features, proof, and calls to action.

best_practices: Use the skill for research and original campaign inspiration.
Save the date, platform, and source for every ad reference.
Validate insights with your own tests before changing campaign strategy.
anti_patterns: Do not copy competitor ad text, screenshots, or designs directly.
Do not scrape platforms in ways that violate access terms.
Do not assume ad frequency proves performance without supporting data.

faq.question: What ad platforms can this skill support?
faq.answer: It is written for public ad libraries such as Facebook Ad Library and LinkedIn ad research workflows.

faq.question: Does it know which ads perform best?
faq.answer: Only when performance data is available. Otherwise, it infers patterns from frequency, format, and messaging.

faq.question: Can it create my own ads?
faq.answer: It can help turn research findings into original angles, but the skill focuses on extraction and analysis.

faq.question: Is it safe to use competitor screenshots?
faq.answer: Use screenshots for internal research. Do not republish or copy protected creative without permission.

faq.question: What outputs can I request?
faq.answer: You can request a markdown report, spreadsheet summary, presentation outline, or categorized pattern library.

faq.question: How often should research be repeated?
faq.answer: Monthly checks are useful for tracking message changes, launches, seasonal campaigns, and new creative tests.
